meta.title: 為什麼叫虛無假設
meta.subtitle: 從一位喝茶的女士到一份聯署聲明：p 值爭議的觀念史
meta.summary: 「虛無」這個譯名，把 null 讀成了「什麼都沒發生」。Fisher 的 null 講的是可推翻性：實驗設下它，就是為了讓資料有東西可以反駁。這篇追一條線——品茶實驗如何生出第一個 p 值，Neyman 與 Pearson 如何把它改成一套決策規則，課本又如何把兩套邏輯焊成一個誰都沒設計過的東西，以及焊接處會裂在哪裡。

en.title: Why It Is Called the Null Hypothesis
en.subtitle: From a lady tasting tea to a signed statement: a short history of the p value quarrel
en.summary: Null was never about nothingness. Fisher's null is the hypothesis that can be nullified — set up so the data have something to argue against. This piece follows one line: how a tea party produced the first p value, how Neyman and Pearson turned it into a decision rule, how textbooks welded the two together into something neither man designed, and where that weld cracks.

lady-tasting-tea.title: 一位喝茶的女士
lady-tasting-tea.description: Fisher 1935 年設計的原型實驗：8 杯奶茶，其中 4 杯先加奶、4 杯先加茶，隨機排列後請品茶者把它們分成兩組各 4 杯。在虛無假設（她其實無法分辨）之下，把全部 C(8,4)=70 種排列窮舉出來，看她答對 0 到 4 杯（猜對「先加奶」組別交集數）的機率各是多少。
lady-tasting-tea.notes: 非模擬，是完全列舉：C(4,k) * C(4,4-k) / C(8,4)，k=0..4。不需要 seed。 / 原始參數註記：{"totalCups":8,"milkFirstCups":4,"method":"hypergeometric exact enumeration, no sampling"}

two-logics.title: 兩套邏輯，一組資料
two-logics.description: 同一組固定資料，分別套用 Fisher 的顯著性檢定邏輯（只問這個 p 值算不算證據）與 Neyman-Pearson 的決策邏輯（事先訂好 alpha，只問拒絕或不拒絕），對照兩套邏輯在同一資料上給出的不同讀法，以及教科書常見的「混血」讀法（拿 p 值又拿固定 alpha，兩套一起用卻沒說清楚在用哪一套）。
two-logics.notes: 資料是寫死的固定值，不是每次重抽。t 與 p 是用零依賴的 pooled t-test 加正規化不完全 beta函數（regularized incomplete beta function）算出來的，算法見 engineering/scripts/verify-simulation-reference.mjs。p 約為 0.0336：alpha=0.05 會拒絕虛無假設，alpha=0.01 不會拒絕，這就是對照兩套邏輯的着力點。不需要 seed。 / 原始參數註記：{"test":"two-sample pooled-variance t-test (Student's t), two-tailed","n1":12,"n2":12,"df":22,"alphaFisher":"報告精確 p 值，不套用固定門檻","alphaNeymanPearson5pct":0.05,"alphaNeymanPearson1pct":0.01,"precomputedT":2.2671894903949465,"precomputedP":0.033550928093355345}

p-under-null.title: 虛無為真時，p 值長什麼樣子
p-under-null.description: 重複從兩組真的沒有差異（或使用者用滑桿調出的固定差異 delta）的常態分布重新抽樣、跑雙樣本 t 檢定，把每次算出的 p 值畫成分布。重點結論：只要虛無假設真的為真（delta=0），p 值不會集中在任何地方，是均勻分布——p=0.03 跟 p=0.83 出現的機率一樣高。
p-under-null.notes: seed=1935 是 Fisher《The Design of Experiments》出版年份，純粹取好記，與統計性質無關。演算法：mulberry32(seed) 依序產生 uniform(0,1) 亂數，每兩個一組經 Box-Muller 轉換成一對標準常態變量；依序填滿 group A（n1 個，N(0,1)）再填滿 group B（n2 個，N(delta,1)）；每個 repeat 重新抽樣、算 pooled t 與雙尾 p；共 repeats 次。決定性演算法，同一 seed 與同一參數必重現同一序列——瀏覽器端實作必須逐步照抄這個抽樣順序，否則會跟 data/reference/null-hypothesis.json 對不上。 / 原始參數註記：{"n1PerGroup":30,"n2PerGroup":30,"deltaSliderRange":[0,1],"deltaDefault":0,"repeats":2000,"alpha":0.05,"test":"two-sample pooled-variance t-test, two-tailed","prng":"mulberry32","normalSampling":"Box-Muller transform on two mulberry32 draws per pair"}

p-hacking.title: 測 20 次，總有一次會亮
p-hacking.description: 同時觀察 20 個互相獨立、且全部真的沒有效應的變數，每個都跑一次雙樣本 t 檢定。就算每個變數各自的假陽性機率只有 5%，只要挑「20 個裡面只要有一個顯著就報告」，整批下來出現至少一個顯著結果的機率遠高於 5%。
p-hacking.notes: seed=2004 是 Gigerenzer, Krauss & Vitouch 出版年份，純粹取好記。每個 trial 依序跑完 20 個變數（每個變數各自抽兩組 N(0,1)、n=nPerGroup），任一變數 p<alpha 就記這個 trial「至少一個顯著」。analyticExpected 是解析解 1-0.95^20，約為 0.6415，供跟模擬比例對照，不是拿來取代模擬結果。 / 原始參數註記：{"nullVariables":20,"nPerGroup":20,"trials":3000,"alpha":0.05,"test":"two-sample pooled-variance t-test, two-tailed","prng":"mulberry32","normalSampling":"Box-Muller transform on two mulberry32 draws per pair","analyticExpected":"1 - (1 - alpha)^nullVariables"}

n-and-effect.title: 樣本數養大，小效應也能顯著
n-and-effect.description: 固定一個很小的真實效應量（Cohen's d=0.15），滑動樣本數 n（每組），看檢定力（power）怎麼隨 n 增加而爬升。重點：統計顯著只等於「偵測到了某個非零效應」，不等於「這個效應大到有意義」——n 夠大，任何非零效應遲早會被偵測到。
n-and-effect.notes: 解析公式，不涉及亂數，不需要 seed。Phi 是標準常態分布的累積分布函數，用 Abramowitz-Stegun 7.1.26 erf 近似式計算，誤差量級 1.5e-7。 / 原始參數註記：{"effectSizeD":0.15,"nSliderRange":[20,2000],"alpha":0.05,"formula":"power = Phi(d * sqrt(n/2) - z_(1-alpha/2))，雙尾檢定、等樣本數兩組的常態近似公式（z_(1-alpha/2)=1.959963985 對應 alpha=0.05）","note":"這是常態近似，不是精確的非中心 t 分布檢定力；教學用途下誤差可忽略，但不宜宣稱為精確值"}

ppv-2x2.title: 顯著結果有多可能是真的
ppv-2x2.description: 把先驗勝算（研究前，這個假設有多可能是真的）、檢定力、alpha 三個輸入放進 Ioannidis (2005) 那套 2x2 陽性預測值（PPV）公式：即使 alpha=0.05、power=0.8 都達標，如果先驗勝算很低，「顯著」結果裡有相當高的比例其實是假陽性。
ppv-2x2.notes: 解析公式，不涉及亂數，不需要 seed。預設值刻意選在教學上有反直覺效果的區間：先驗機率換算後很低時，就算 alpha 與 power 都是教科書標準值，PPV 也遠低於直覺預期的 95%。 / 原始參數註記：{"priorOddsDefault":0.1,"powerDefault":0.8,"alphaDefault":0.05,"formula":"PPV = (power * R) / (power * R + alpha)，R 是 Ioannidis (2005) 定義的先驗勝算（pre-study odds），直接代入即可，不要再把它換算成機率——這是最容易寫錯的一步","precomputedDefaultPPV":0.6153846153846154}

fisher1935DesignOfExperiments.author: Ronald A. Fisher
fisher1935DesignOfExperiments.title: The Design of Experiments
fisher1935DesignOfExperiments.locator: Ch. II, §5 'The Statement of Experiment', p.13（茶品實驗設定：8 杯，4 杯先加奶）；§8 'The Null Hypothesis'，p.18（1935 初版；部分刷次標為 p.19）。
fisher1935DesignOfExperiments.quote: In relation to any experiment we may speak of this hypothesis as the null hypothesis, and it should be noted that the null hypothesis is never proved or established, but is possibly disproved, in the course of experimentation. Every experiment may be said to exist only in order to give the facts a chance of disproving the null hypothesis.

en.locator: Ch. II §5 'The Statement of Experiment', p.13 (the tea-tasting setup); §8 'The Null Hypothesis', p.18 (1935 first edition; some reprints give p.19).
en.quote: In relation to any experiment we may speak of this hypothesis as the null hypothesis, and it should be noted that the null hypothesis is never proved or established, but is possibly disproved, in the course of experimentation. Every experiment may be said to exist only in order to give the facts a chance of disproving the null hypothesis.

fisher1925StatisticalMethods.author: Ronald A. Fisher
fisher1925StatisticalMethods.title: Statistical Methods for Research Workers
fisher1925StatisticalMethods.locator: 13th ed.（1958 印次系列），p.44（部分版本標為 p.47，因版本／刷次不同）。
fisher1925StatisticalMethods.quote: The value for which P=0.05, or 1 in 20, is 1.96 or nearly 2; it is convenient to take this point as a limit in judging whether a deviation ought to be considered significant or not.

en.locator: 13th ed. (1958 printing series), p.44 (some editions give p.47, reflecting differences between printings).
en.quote: The value for which P=0.05, or 1 in 20, is 1.96 or nearly 2; it is convenient to take this point as a limit in judging whether a deviation ought to be considered significant or not.

neymanPearson1933.author: Jerzy Neyman and Egon S. Pearson
neymanPearson1933.title: On the Problem of the Most Efficient Tests of Statistical Hypotheses
neymanPearson1933.container: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society of London, Series A, vol. 231, pp.289–337
neymanPearson1933.locator: p.291（「rule of behaviour」段落，決策式邏輯的原始表述）；pp.296–297（H0 記號、「errors of the first kind」與第二類錯誤用語首次系統性提出之處）。「power」一詞本身在這篇論文中尚未以此固定術語出現。
neymanPearson1933.quote: If we reject H0, we may reject it when it is true; if we accept H0, we may be accepting it when it is false, that is to say, when really some alternative H_t is true. These two sources of error can rarely be eliminated completely... In trying to choose a proper critical region, we notice at once that it is very easy to control errors of the first kind referred to above.

en.locator: p.291 (the 'rule of behaviour' passage, the original statement of the decision-theoretic logic); pp.296-297 (the notation H0, and the first systematic statement of what became Type I and Type II error). The word 'power' itself is not yet used as a fixed term in this paper.
en.quote: If we reject H0, we may reject it when it is true; if we accept H0, we may be accepting it when it is false, that is to say, when really some alternative H_t is true. These two sources of error can rarely be eliminated completely... In trying to choose a proper critical region, we notice at once that it is very easy to control errors of the first kind referred to above.

gigerenzerKraussVitouch2004NullRitual.author: Gerd Gigerenzer, Stefan Krauss, and Oliver Vitouch
gigerenzerKraussVitouch2004NullRitual.title: The Null Ritual: What You Always Wanted to Know About Significance Testing but Were Afraid to Ask
gigerenzerKraussVitouch2004NullRitual.container: In D. Kaplan (Ed.), The Sage Handbook of Quantitative Methodology for the Social Sciences, pp.391–408. Thousand Oaks, CA: Sage
gigerenzerKraussVitouch2004NullRitual.locator: p.392（「虛無儀式」三步驟定義；六題是非題示範 p 值的六種常見誤讀，全部為 False）
gigerenzerKraussVitouch2004NullRitual.quote: The essence of the ritual is the following: (1) Set up a statistical null hypothesis of "no mean difference" or "zero correlation." Don't specify the predictions of your research hypothesis or of any alternative substantive hypotheses. (2) Use 5% as a convention for rejecting the null. If significant, accept your research hypothesis. (3) Always perform this procedure.

en.locator: p.392 (the three-step definition of the 'null ritual'; six true/false items demonstrating six common misreadings of the p-value, all in fact false).
en.quote: The essence of the ritual is the following: (1) Set up a statistical null hypothesis of "no mean difference" or "zero correlation." Don't specify the predictions of your research hypothesis or of any alternative substantive hypotheses. (2) Use 5% as a convention for rejecting the null. If significant, accept your research hypothesis. (3) Always perform this procedure.

gigerenzerKraussVitouch2004NullEtymology.author: Gerd Gigerenzer, Stefan Krauss, and Oliver Vitouch
gigerenzerKraussVitouch2004NullEtymology.title: The Null Ritual: What You Always Wanted to Know About Significance Testing but Were Afraid to Ask
gigerenzerKraussVitouch2004NullEtymology.container: In D. Kaplan (Ed.), The Sage Handbook of Quantitative Methodology for the Social Sciences, pp.391–408. Thousand Oaks, CA: Sage
gigerenzerKraussVitouch2004NullEtymology.locator: p.392（Gigerenzer 等對 Fisher 命名「null」原意的詮釋性重述，非辭典級語源條目）。
gigerenzerKraussVitouch2004NullEtymology.quote: Some suggest that it was authorized by the eminent statistician Sir Ronald A. Fisher, owing to the emphasis on null hypothesis testing (not to be confused with the null ritual) in his 1935 book. However, Fisher would have rejected all three ingredients of this procedure. First, null does not refer to a zero mean difference or correlation but to the hypothesis to be "nullified," which could postulate a correlation of .3, for instance.

en.locator: p.392 (Gigerenzer et al.'s interpretive account of Fisher's original intent behind the term "null"; not a dictionary-grade etymology entry).
en.quote: Some suggest that it was authorized by the eminent statistician Sir Ronald A. Fisher, owing to the emphasis on null hypothesis testing (not to be confused with the null ritual) in his 1935 book. However, Fisher would have rejected all three ingredients of this procedure. First, null does not refer to a zero mean difference or correlation but to the hypothesis to be "nullified," which could postulate a correlation of .3, for instance.

wassersteinLazar2016.author: Ronald L. Wasserstein and Nicole A. Lazar
wassersteinLazar2016.title: The ASA Statement on p-Values: Context, Process, and Purpose
wassersteinLazar2016.container: The American Statistician, vol. 70, no. 2, pp.129–133
wassersteinLazar2016.locator: 六條原則列表，正文 p.131（"Principle 1" 起）
wassersteinLazar2016.quote: P-values do not measure the probability that the studied hypothesis is true, or the probability that the data were produced by random chance alone.

en.locator: The six principles, listed starting p.131 (from "Principle 1").
en.quote: P-values do not measure the probability that the studied hypothesis is true, or the probability that the data were produced by random chance alone.

greenland2016.author: Sander Greenland, Stephen J. Senn, Kenneth J. Rothman, John B. Carlin, Charles Poole, Steven N. Goodman, and Douglas G. Altman
greenland2016.title: Statistical Tests, P Values, Confidence Intervals, and Power: A Guide to Misinterpretations
greenland2016.container: European Journal of Epidemiology, vol. 31, no. 4, pp.337–350
greenland2016.locator: §2「常見誤讀清單」，列舉 25 條——正文標題即為 "Twenty-five misinterpretations" 對應段落
greenland2016.quote: Misinterpretation and abuse of statistical tests, confidence intervals, and statistical power have been decried for decades, yet remain rampant.

en.locator: §2, the list of 25 common misinterpretations, corresponding to the section headed "Twenty-five misinterpretations."
en.quote: Misinterpretation and abuse of statistical tests, confidence intervals, and statistical power have been decried for decades, yet remain rampant.

richardson2021LadyTastingTea.author: John T. E. Richardson
richardson2021LadyTastingTea.title: A Closer Look at the Lady Tasting Tea
richardson2021LadyTastingTea.locator: 全文——重新檢視 Fisher《The Design of Experiments》對茶品實驗的原始記載，比對 Joan Fisher Box（1978，Fisher 之女所寫傳記）與 David Salsburg（1968，轉引 Hugh Fairfield-Smith 數十年後的回憶）兩種不同的事後轉述。
richardson2021LadyTastingTea.quote: Fisher 本人在《The Design of Experiments》中只描述了實驗設計本身（8 杯，4 杯先加奶），從未記載這位女士（Muriel Bristol）實際的作答結果。「全部答對」的說法源自 Salsburg 1968 年轉述 Hugh Fairfield-Smith 數十年後的回憶；Fisher 的女兒 Joan Fisher Box 在傳記中給出的版本保守得多——她「答對的杯數足以證明她的說法」，未必是滿分。Richardson 認為 Box 的版本較為可信，若真如此，答對數更可能是 6/8（p=0.243），在傳統顯著水準（0.05）下並不顯著。

en.locator: Full article — re-examines Fisher's original account of the tea-tasting experiment in The Design of Experiments against two later retellings: Joan Fisher Box's 1978 biography of her father, and David Salsburg's 1968 account relaying Hugh Fairfield-Smith's decades-later recollection.
en.quote: Fisher's own account in The Design of Experiments describes only the design (eight cups, four milk-first) and never records how the lady (Muriel Bristol) actually performed. The claim that she got all eight right traces to Salsburg's 1968 retelling of Hugh Fairfield-Smith's decades-later recollection; Fisher's daughter Joan Fisher Box, in her biography, gives a more modest account — that the lady 'divined correctly more than enough of those cups... to prove her case,' not necessarily a perfect score. Richardson argues Box's version is the more credible one; on that reading the actual tally may have been six of eight correct (p = 0.243), not significant at the conventional 0.05 level.

gigerenzer2004MindlessStatistics.author: Gerd Gigerenzer
gigerenzer2004MindlessStatistics.title: Mindless Statistics
gigerenzer2004MindlessStatistics.container: The Journal of Socio-Economics, vol. 33, no. 5, pp.587–606
gigerenzer2004MindlessStatistics.locator: p.591–592：以金屬板製造商的品管情境為例，逐步示範 Neyman–Pearson 決策邏輯（設定第一類、第二類錯誤率、依成本效益抓樣本數，「接受」不等於「相信」）如何脫胎於工業品管的驗收邏輯。
gigerenzer2004MindlessStatistics.quote: Neyman–Pearson 檢定的典型應用是品質管制。設想一位醫療器材金屬板的製造商：她認為兩類錯誤的代價不對稱——誤報（false alarm）代價較小，漏抓故障（miss）代價較大，因此把第一類錯誤率設得很小、第二類錯誤率設得較大，並據此算出每天須抽驗的樣本數。當她「接受」機器故障這個假設時，她是依此行動、停止生產，但這不代表她相信機器真的故障。

en.locator: pp.591-592: works through a metal-plate manufacturer's quality-control scenario to show how Neyman-Pearson decision logic (setting Type I/II error rates, choosing sample size by cost-benefit trade-off, "accepting" a hypothesis without believing it) grew out of industrial acceptance-sampling logic.
en.quote: A typical application of Neyman–Pearson testing is in quality control. Imagine a manufacturer of metal plates that are used in medical instruments... She considers false alarms, that is, accepting H2 while H1 is true, to be the less serious error, and misses of malfunctioning... to be more serious... She now calculates the required sample size n of plates that must be sampled every day to test the quality of the production... When she accepts H2, she acts as if there were a malfunction and stops production, but this does not mean that she believes that H2 is true.

simmonsNelsonSimonsohn2011FalsePositive.author: Joseph P. Simmons, Leif D. Nelson, and Uri Simonsohn
simmonsNelsonSimonsohn2011FalsePositive.title: False-Positive Psychology: Undisclosed Flexibility in Data Collection and Analysis Allows Presenting Anything as Significant
simmonsNelsonSimonsohn2011FalsePositive.locator: Study 2：20 名賓州大學大學生分兩組聆聽披頭四〈When I'm Sixty-Four〉或對照曲〈Kalimba〉，再回報出生年月與父親年齡；聽披頭四組經統計校正後平均年齡 20.1 歲，對照組 21.5 歲，F(1,17)=4.92，p=.040。文中另以模擬顯示：容許研究者在資料蒐集與分析上有彈性，可把單一比較的名目 5% 偽陽性率推高到 60.7%。
simmonsNelsonSimonsohn2011FalsePositive.quote: 根據回報的出生日期，聽過〈When I'm Sixty-Four〉的受試者比聽對照曲的受試者年輕了近一歲半（校正後平均 20.1 歲 vs. 21.5 歲），F(1, 17) = 4.92，p = .040。

en.locator: Study 2: 20 University of Pennsylvania undergraduates listened to either the Beatles' "When I'm Sixty-Four" or a control song ("Kalimba"), then reported their birth date and father's age; adjusted mean age was 20.1 in the Beatles condition versus 21.5 in the control condition, F(1,17) = 4.92, p = .040. The paper's simulations separately show that allowing flexibility in data collection and analysis can push a single comparison's nominal 5% false-positive rate up to 60.7%.
en.quote: According to their reported birth date, people were nearly a year-and-a-half younger after listening to "When I'm Sixty-Four" (adjusted M = 20.1 years) rather than to "Kalimba" (adjusted M = 21.5 years), F(1, 17) = 4.92, p = .040.

openScienceCollaboration2015Reproducibil.author: Open Science Collaboration
openScienceCollaboration2015Reproducibil.title: Estimating the Reproducibility of Psychological Science
openScienceCollaboration2015Reproducibil.locator: 重做 100 篇心理學實證研究（原刊於三份心理學期刊）：原研究中 97% 曾報告達統計顯著（p<.05）；重做後僅 36% 的研究再度達到統計顯著，重做效果量平均只有原研究的一半左右。
openScienceCollaboration2015Reproducibil.quote: Although statistically significant results were reported in 97% of original studies, only 36% of replications yielded statistically significant results.

en.locator: Replicated 100 empirical psychology studies (originally published in three journals): 97% of the original studies had reported statistically significant results (p < .05); only 36% of the replications reached statistical significance, and replication effect sizes were on average about half the magnitude of the original effect sizes.
en.quote: Although statistically significant results were reported in 97% of original studies, only 36% of replications yielded statistically significant results.

steeringCommittee1988PhysiciansHealthStu.author: Steering Committee of the Physicians' Health Study Research Group
steeringCommittee1988PhysiciansHealthStu.title: Preliminary Report: Findings from the Aspirin Component of the Ongoing Physicians' Health Study
steeringCommittee1988PhysiciansHealthStu.container: New England Journal of Medicine, vol. 318, no. 4, pp.262–264
steeringCommittee1988PhysiciansHealthStu.locator: 22,071 名男性醫師隨機分派服用阿斯匹靈（11,037 人）或安慰劑（11,034 人）；追蹤期間阿斯匹靈組 104 人、安慰劑組 189 人發生心肌梗塞，相對風險約 0.56（降低 44%），因效果極端顯著而提前終止試驗。

en.locator: 22,071 male physicians randomized to aspirin (n=11,037) or placebo (n=11,034); 104 myocardial infarctions occurred in the aspirin group versus 189 in the placebo group, relative risk approximately 0.56 (a 44% reduction) — the effect was extreme enough that the trial's aspirin component was stopped early.

rosenthal1990SoftPsychology.author: Robert Rosenthal
rosenthal1990SoftPsychology.title: How Are We Doing in Soft Psychology?
rosenthal1990SoftPsychology.locator: 以二元效果量顯示法（Binomial Effect Size Display, BESD）重新詮釋阿斯匹靈與心肌梗塞試驗的效果量：相關係數 r 僅約 .03，量級很小，但換算成「每若干人中避免幾次心肌梗塞」的實際人數後，效果並不能說是無足輕重。

en.locator: Reinterprets the aspirin/myocardial-infarction trial's effect size using the Binomial Effect Size Display (BESD): a correlation of r approximately .03 is small in magnitude, but translated into "heart attacks prevented per N people," the practical effect is not negligible.

ioannidis2005WhyMostPublished.author: John P. A. Ioannidis
ioannidis2005WhyMostPublished.title: Why Most Published Research Findings Are False
ioannidis2005WhyMostPublished.locator: 全文——以陽性預測值（PPV）模型說明：在事前機率低、樣本小、分析彈性大的研究領域，多數「達統計顯著」的發表結果其實是偽陽性。
ioannidis2005WhyMostPublished.quote: There is increasing concern that most current published research findings are false. It can be proven that most claimed research findings are false.

en.locator: Full article — uses a positive predictive value (PPV) model to argue that in fields with low prior probability, small samples, and flexible analysis practices, most published statistically significant findings are in fact false positives.
en.quote: There is increasing concern that most current published research findings are false. It can be proven that most claimed research findings are false.

quiz.prompt: 一個檢定算出 p=0.03。這代表什麼？
options: 虛無假設為真的機率只有 3%
如果虛無假設為真，得到目前這組資料（或更極端資料）的機率是 3%
研究假設為真的機率有 97%
這個結果有 97% 的機率可以被重複驗證
quiz.explain: p 值的定義是「條件在虛無假設為真之上，觀察到目前資料或更極端資料的機率」，寫成符號是 p(D|H0)。它不是任何一個假設為真或為假的機率——要問假設為真的機率，需要貝氏方法帶入先驗，單靠 p 值算不出來。Gigerenzer, Krauss & Vitouch (2004) 的六題是非題示範就是專門打這個誤解。

quiz.prompt: 一個效應在統計上顯著（p<0.05），代表這個效應在實際意義上也夠大、值得關心嗎？
options: 對，顯著就代表效應夠大
不一定，樣本數夠大時，極小的效應也會顯著
只有 p<0.01 才代表效應夠大
顯著程度和效應大小是同一件事，數學上等價
quiz.explain: 統計顯著只回答「這個效應是不是剛好等於零」，不回答「這個效應大不大」。n 越大，越小的真實效應也越容易被偵測出來——這正是 n-and-effect 那個互動元件想示範的：固定一個很小的效應量 d=0.15，把 n 從 20 拉到 2000，檢定力會一路爬升，即使效應大小從頭到尾沒變過。

quiz.prompt: 同時檢定 20 個彼此獨立、且全部真的沒有效應的變數，每個都用 alpha=0.05。整批下來，至少出現一個「顯著」結果的機率大約是多少？
options: 5%，因為每個變數各自的門檻就是 5%
100%，因為測得夠多一定會中
大約 64%
0%，因為全部變數真的沒有效應
quiz.explain: 每個變數各自的假陽性機率是 5%，但 20 個獨立變數全部都不顯著的機率是 0.95^20 約 0.36，所以至少一個顯著的機率約為 1-0.95^20，約 64%。這就是 p-hacking 那個互動元件用模擬示範的數字，本檔案也用解析公式與模擬雙重驗證過。

quiz.prompt: Fisher 的顯著性檢定邏輯，跟 Neyman-Pearson 的決策邏輯，最核心的差異是什麼？
options: 兩者其實是同一套方法，只是用詞不同
Fisher 只問這次資料提供多少「證據」去質疑虛無假設；Neyman-Pearson 事先訂好 alpha，把每次檢定當成一條長期不會太常出錯的決策規則，不對單次結果做證據強弱的解讀
Neyman-Pearson 邏輯不需要虛無假設，只有 Fisher 的邏輯需要
Fisher 邏輯只能用在小樣本，Neyman-Pearson 邏輯只能用在大樣本
quiz.explain: Fisher 1935 年的立場是「虛無假設從不被證明，只可能被推翻」，p 值報告的是這次資料構成多強的反對證據。Neyman 與 Pearson 1933 年的論文則是另一套語言：先訂好長期犯錯率（第一類、第二類錯誤），把檢定當成一條「規則」（rule of behaviour），不對單一結果做證據強弱的解讀，只問要不要照這條規則行動。今天教科書里常見的「用 p 值報告、又套一個固定 0.05 門檻」，是兩套邏輯焊接出來、誰都没有正式設計過的混血程序——Gigerenzer 等人管這個叫 hybrid logic。

quiz.prompt: 2016 年美國統計學會（ASA）發表〈p 值聲明〉，最主要的立場是什麼？
options: 呼籲把顯著門檻從 0.05 改成 0.005
宣布 p 值完全沒用，應該全面廢除
列出一組原則，指出 p 值本身不能單獨決定一個科學結論是否成立、也不等於效應大小或重要性
只是重申 alpha=0.05 是唯一正確的門檻
quiz.explain: ASA 聲明（Wasserstein & Lazar, 2016）沒有廢除 p 值，也沒有指定新門檻，而是列出六條原則，說明 p 值能回答什麼、不能回答什麼——例如它不是虛無假設為真的機率，也不能單獨拿來判斷一個科學或商業決策是否成立。這份聲明本身也成為後續一系列「後 p<0.05 時代」討論的起點，包括 Greenland 等人（2016）那份列出 25 條常見誤讀的清單。

misreadings.claim: p = 0.03 代表虛無假設為真的機率只有 3%
misreadings.why: p 值算的是「虛無為真的前提下，看到這種資料的機率」。這句話把條件反過來讀成「看到這種資料的前提下，虛無為真的機率」。兩者可以差很遠，差多遠取決於這個領域裡本來有多少假說是真的——而 p 值對此一無所知。
misreadings.instead: 要回答「這個結果有多大機會是真的」，必須額外引入事前勝算與檢定力（見本文的 2x2 表）。

en.claim: p = 0.03 means there is only a 3% chance the null is true
en.why: A p value is the probability of data like these given that the null holds. This reads the condition backwards. How far apart the two numbers are depends on how many hypotheses in the field were true to begin with, and the p value knows nothing about that.
en.instead: To ask how likely a finding is to be real, you have to bring in prior odds and power (see the 2x2 table above).

misreadings.claim: p 越小，對立假設為真的機率越大
misreadings.why: 同一個條件方向的錯誤。p 值的計算從頭到尾只用到虛無假設，對立假設沒有進入這個算式。
misreadings.instead: 把對立假設的可信度納入計算，需要的是貝氏因子或事後機率，那是另一套工具。

en.claim: The smaller the p, the more likely the alternative is true
en.why: Same reversal. The alternative hypothesis never enters the calculation of a p value; only the null does.
en.instead: Weighing the credibility of the alternative needs a Bayes factor or a posterior, which is a different tool.

misreadings.why: 沒有拒絕虛無假設，只代表這批資料沒有提供足夠的理由去推翻它。樣本太小、變異太大、測量太粗，都會讓真實存在的效果過不了門檻。虛無假設從不被證明。
misreadings.instead: 報告效果量與信賴區間；若真要正面宣稱「差距小到不重要」，用等價檢定，並事先講清楚多小算不重要。

en.claim: p > 0.05, so there is no effect
en.why: Failing to reject means the data gave you no good reason to reject. A small sample, a noisy measure, or high variance will hide a real effect. The null is never proved.
en.instead: Report the effect size and interval. If you want to claim the difference is negligible, run an equivalence test and say in advance what negligible means.

misreadings.claim: p 值越小，效果越大、越重要
misreadings.why: p 值同時受效果大小與樣本數影響。樣本夠大時，任何微不足道的差距都能得到極小的 p 值。
misreadings.instead: 效果大小要看效果量本身（差值、比值、標準化效果量），不看 p。

en.claim: A smaller p means a bigger, more important effect
en.why: The p value moves with both effect size and sample size. With a large enough sample, a trivial difference produces a tiny p.
en.instead: Size lives in the effect size — a difference, a ratio, a standardized effect — not in p.

misreadings.why: 0.05 是 Fisher 順手取的一個方便界線，沒有理論地位。它之所以流傳，一部分原因是當年查表方便。
misreadings.instead: 門檻的高低該由這個決策的代價決定：偽陽性有多貴、偽陰性有多貴。

en.why: Fisher picked one in twenty as a convenient line, partly because it made the tables usable. There is no theory behind it.
en.instead: Let the cost of the decision set the threshold: how expensive is a false positive, how expensive is a false negative.

misreadings.claim: p = 0.01 代表重做這個研究有 99% 的機會再次顯著
misreadings.why: 重複研究的成功率取決於真實效果與檢定力，跟這一次的 p 值不是同一回事。單次 p 值對複製機率的資訊量很低。
misreadings.instead: 要談複製，先談檢定力與效果量的估計精度。

en.claim: p = 0.01 means a replication has a 99% chance of coming out significant again
en.why: Replication success depends on the true effect and the power of the study, not on this one p value, which carries very little information about it.
en.instead: Talk about power and the precision of the effect estimate instead.

timeline.title: Fisher 出版《研究工作者的統計方法》
timeline.body: 顯著性檢定的操作手冊。二十分之一被說成一個方便的界線，日後成為 0.05。

en.title: Fisher publishes Statistical Methods for Research Workers
en.body: The working manual of significance testing. One in twenty is called a convenient line to draw; it becomes 0.05.

timeline.title: Neyman 與 Pearson 提出最有效檢定
timeline.body: H0 與 H1 的配對、第一類與第二類錯誤、臨界域。統計檢定被改寫成一套關於長期行為的決策規則。

en.title: Neyman and Pearson give the most efficient test
en.body: H0 paired with H1, errors of the first and second kind, a critical region. Testing is rewritten as a rule about long-run behaviour.

timeline.title: Fisher 出版《實驗設計》
timeline.body: 女士品茶登場。虛無假設被明確界定為那個可能被否證、卻永遠不會被證明的假設。

en.title: Fisher publishes The Design of Experiments
en.body: The lady tasting tea. The null hypothesis is defined as the one that may be disproved and is never proved.

timeline.title: Fisher 與 Neyman 的長期爭執
timeline.body: 兩人在期刊與講台上互相攻擊。爭的是統計推論到底在做什麼：衡量證據，還是規範行為。

en.title: Fisher against Neyman, for twenty years
en.body: They attack each other in journals and from lecterns. The quarrel is about what inference is for: weighing evidence, or governing behaviour.

timeline.title: 教科書把兩套邏輯縫起來
timeline.body: 心理學與社會科學的方法教科書開始教一套混合程序：報告 p 值，同時宣稱長期錯誤率的保證。兩位當事人都不會承認這是自己的東西。

en.title: Textbooks weld the two logics together
en.body: Methods texts in psychology and the social sciences teach a hybrid: report the p value, and claim the long-run error guarantee. Neither man would have signed it.

timeline.title: Gigerenzer 稱之為 null ritual
timeline.body: 這套程序被指認為一種儀式：機械執行、沒有作者、也沒有人願意為它的邏輯辯護。

en.title: Gigerenzer names it the null ritual
en.body: A procedure performed mechanically, with no author, and no one willing to defend its logic.

timeline.title: 複製危機
timeline.body: 大規模重做實驗的計畫顯示，大量已發表的顯著結果重做不出來。p-hacking、發表偏誤、低檢定力被指認為結構性原因。

en.title: The replication crisis
en.body: Large replication projects fail to reproduce a great many published significant results. p-hacking, publication bias and low power are identified as structural causes.

timeline.title: 美國統計學會發表 p 值聲明
timeline.body: 六條原則。最直白的一條：科學結論不該只建立在 p 值是否越過某個門檻上。同年 Greenland 等人列出二十五條常見誤讀。

en.title: The ASA issues a statement on p values
en.body: Six principles. The bluntest: scientific conclusions should not rest on whether a p value clears a threshold. The same year, Greenland and colleagues list twenty-five misreadings.
